title: taraxacum anomum
intro: Taraxacum anomum, also known as the common dandelion, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion genus and is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum anomum lie in its leaves, roots, and flowers, which contain compounds like taraxasterol, sesquiterpene lactones, and flavonoids. The health benefits of the plant include its use in treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting liver and kidney function, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum anomum include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from the leaves, roots, and flowers, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
